title: dryopteris pusilla
intro: Dryopteris pusilla, also known as the dwarf wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus. It is a small to medium-sized fern with fronds that typically grow to a height of 2-8 inches and is native to various regions of Asia, Europe, and North America. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris pusilla include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and an antioxidant, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, and it has been reported to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system.
